blocks.text: `mac-app-store` separates the sandboxed Store App from a bundle-internal helper/login item. The Store App owns onboarding and controls; the helper owns local-only IPC and task execution orchestration.

items: The helper role is `sandboxed-login-item-helper` and startup registration uses `SMAppService` / Login Item style mechanisms.
Background execution requires both `background-helper` consent and Store review approval.
Direct LaunchAgent install, self-updater, shared-location install, standalone code download, and provider CLI bundling are forbidden.
App data root must be an app group or sandbox container; user home Application Support fallback is rejected.
Local IPC is a helper-owned Unix domain socket under the app group or sandbox container root.
Workspace access reaches C6 only when a security-scoped grant and `workspace-access:<workspace-id>` consent are both active.

Review notes must explain helper purpose, Login Item consent UX, App Sandbox entitlement use, security-scoped workspace grants, provider CLI non-bundling, review/demo mode, and privacy metadata allowlisting.

blocks.text: `internal/hostintegration.ResolveHelperRuntimePlan` computes the role, startup registration, background rule, workspace grant requirement, update rule, and review-note surfaces. OS API calls, entitlements, and bookmark bytes remain adapter-owned.
